summary: exxpress-utils typosquat exfiltrates npm/git credentials and crypto wallet data to hardcoded C2 on install
details: The package name is a typosquat of express-utils/express (doubled 'x'), with keywords ('lodash', 'utilities') and a generic description designed to attract mistaken installs. index.js is a stub labeled by the author as 'Just a dummy module. The real payload is in postinstall.js'. On npm install, postinstall.js (wired via package.json scripts.postinstall = 'node postinstall.js') performs two classes of installer harm: (1) Credential harvest — reads ~/.npmrc (extracting _authToken and npm_ tokens that grant publish rights to the installer's npm account), ~/.env (regex-matching values for tokens, secrets, API keys, DB/cloud/payment/email/webhook credentials), and ~/.git-credentials. (2) Crypto wallet theft — enumerates 71 hardcoded Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium/Vivaldi/Opera wallet extension IDs (MetaMask, Phantom, Coinbase Wallet, Trust Wallet, etc.) under Local Extension Settings, reads LevelDB logs, regex-matches vault/mnemonic/seed/privateKey fields, then walks ~/Documents, ~/Desktop, ~/Downloads for filenames matching crypto/seed/wallet/key keywords and bodies containing BIP39 words. The collected data is JSON-POSTed over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at 149.28.127.35:8888. The file's own header comment self-describes as 'Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner — Runs on npm install. Silent. Zero trace.'

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
